content.claim: Hungarian school financing operates on a capitation (per-capita) basis; schools that lose high-performing students to 6/8-year gimnáziums lose both the student's funding and the positive peer effects that high-performing peers provide to remaining students, with no compensatory funding mechanism documented for feeder schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: The claim about absence of compensatory mechanisms is inferred from financing rules; explicit confirmation that no hidden adjustment exists is not documented
Peer-effect magnitude and heterogeneity by school context are not quantified in the source materials
The opportunity cost to remaining students is theoretically sound but not empirically measured for Hungary specifically
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
